kicker: ET LEVEL I // EDDY CURRENT DISTRIBUTION

beats.narration: Once a coil induces current in a part, three properties decide what your test can see — and where it goes blind.

payload.title: WHERE THE CURRENTS ARE
payload.sub: Three properties set what ET can and cannot detect.

beats.narration: First, they travel in closed circular loops. Second, they crowd at the surface. Third, in a solid bar they fall to zero at the center.
They crowd at the surface, each layer shielding the metal below — density decays exponentially: thirty-seven percent at one standard depth, five percent at three.
And a flaw only signals when it interrupts the loops. A crack across them detours the current; one lying parallel barely shows.
So ET lives on the surface. A solid product's core is invisible — that's physics, not a setting you can fix.

payload.sub: Eddy current testing is a surface method by physics.
